news.title: With 283 ‘dangerous’ buildings, Ulhasnagar braces for rain fury
news.para: Ulhasnagar Municipal Corporation's pre-monsoon survey identified 283 dangerous buildings, with six declared extremely hazardous and requiring immediate evacuation. A significant number of structures need major or urgent repairs, and 144 lack essential structural audit reports, raising concerns about their stability as monsoon approaches.

news.title: Chandigarh administration may allow industrial plot fragmentation for four kanal units
news.para: Chandigarh's UT administration has proposed allowing fragmentation of industrial plots measuring 4 kanal and above, with a maximum of three subdivisions and a minimum plot width of 50 feet. This move aims to ensure regulated development, preventing colony-like layouts and mandating adequate parking. Revised zoning plans will be required before construction.

news.title: Chennai's town planning department to issue building permits within 27 days
news.para: Chennai's town planning department will now issue building permits within 27 days, aiming to curb corruption and delays. Officials are barred from summoning applicants, with all processes, including payments and clarifications, moving online. This move ensures time-bound, transparent service delivery for the public, streamlining approvals for various building types.

news.title: May 31 last date to pay property tax, avail rebate: Chandigarh civic body
news.para: Property owners in Chandigarh have a final chance to save money on their taxes. The municipal corporation is urging everyone to pay by May 31 to secure a 20% rebate on residential and 10% on commercial property tax. Failure to meet this deadline will result in a hefty 25% penalty plus 12% interest.

news.title: Mumbai property registrations rise 7% to 12,315 units in May 2026: Knight Frank
news.para: Mumbai recorded 12,315 property registrations in May 2026, up seven per cent year-on-year, marking the highest registrations for the month of May in 14 years, according to Knight Frank India. Stamp duty collections stood at ₹1,051 crore during the month, marginally lower than the year-ago period.

news.title: Irregularities in Solan housing project under Himachal Pradesh HC scanner
news.para: Himachal Pradesh High Court has issued notices to state and central governments, CBI, and ED. This is regarding alleged illegalities in the Chester Hills housing project. The project is on nearly 275 bighas of agricultural land. Allegations involve violations of land transfer laws. A report highlighted concerns about non-agriculturist promoters' control.

news.title: Telangana HC questions HYDRAA over demolitions in Kokapet without notice
news.para: The Telangana High Court has questioned HYDRAA's demolition activities at ZRESTA Villas without prior notice, issuing interim orders for status quo. Petitioners claim their compound wall was illegally razed on May 26. The court emphasized that demolishing structures while land is under dispute, especially without issuing notices, is unacceptable.

news.title: Tamil Nadu lorry owners allege corruption, seek digital reforms & policies for M-sand
news.para: Tamil Nadu Sand Lorry Owners Federation urged the state government to implement an E-Way Bill system for the mining sector to curb illegal mineral transportation and corruption. They also called for a manufactured sand (M-sand) policy for crusher units, estimating potential annual revenue generation of up to ₹50,000 crore.

news.title: Coastal realty faces shortage of 3BHK apartments
news.para: Coastal real estate stakeholders report a significant mismatch between buyer demand and developer supply, particularly for 3BHK apartments under Rs 1 crore. While demand for larger homes has surged post-pandemic, new launches haven't kept pace, leading to shortages. Conversely, the premium segment faces oversupply, with rising rents attributed to limited availability in sought-after configurations and locations.

news.title: Ghaziabad development body to hold review meet over repeated fires in societies
news.para: Ghaziabad administration is investigating two recent fires in Indirapuram. Reports are being gathered from Ghaziabad Development Authority, the electricity provider, and the fire department. A meeting is scheduled to review these findings. The focus is on fire safety measures and emergency preparedness in residential and commercial complexes. Action will be taken based on the submitted reports.

news.title: Calcutta HC stays demolition of Tiljala, Kasba buildings for a month
news.para: The Calcutta High Court has halted demolition notices for buildings in Tiljala and Kasba for 30 days. The Kolkata Municipal Corporation must now hear from both building owners and their tenants. This order follows petitions challenging demolition notices pasted on building walls without formal notification to occupants.

news.title: Delhi govt's smart streetlight project to save ₹25 crore annually in power expense
news.para: Delhi's streetlights are undergoing a massive LED conversion, aiming to slash electricity consumption by 3.5 crore units annually. This initiative, costing Rs 473.24 crore, will also establish a control center for real-time monitoring and enable adaptive lighting, projecting significant cost savings over five years.

news.title: MHADA identifies 82 highly dangerous buildings in Mumbai city island
news.para: MHADA’s Mumbai Building Repairs and Reconstruction Board has identified 82 highly dangerous cessed buildings in its annual pre-monsoon survey of old and dilapidated structures on Mumbai city island. These buildings house 2,736 occupants, including 2,256 residential and 480 non-residential tenants.

news.title: NMMC has authority to collect property tax in Thane-Belapur industrial belt: SC
news.para: The Supreme Court has ruled that the Navi Mumbai Municipal Corporation can collect property tax from industries in the Thane Trans Creek-MIDC area. This decision settles a long-standing dispute. The court clarified the difference between taxes and fees, empowering NMMC to levy taxes. This will boost city development with over Rs 800 crore in pending dues.

news.title: Telangana: Raidurg land fetches ₹5,000 crore in eight months
news.para: Raidurg, Hyderabad's Knowledge City, has generated over Rs 5,000 crore for Telangana in just eight months. High-value land auctions have made this two-square-kilometre area the state's most valuable real estate hotspot. IT majors and corporate giants are anchoring projects here. Kokapet is the second major hotspot. Raidurg's land valuations are extraordinary.

news.title: CREDAI Visakhapatnam seeks simplified registration for large housing projects under CARD 2.0
news.para: CREDAI Visakhapatnam has urged the stamps and registration department to implement a project ID-based framework under CARD 2.0. This initiative aims to streamline registration for large residential projects by reducing repetitive data entry and verification processes. The proposed system would enhance efficiency and speed up registrations for developers and applicants.

news.title: Andhra Pradesh: ₹3,500 crore mega furniture manufacturing cluster planned near Gudur
news.para: Nellore district authorities have identified 1,000 acres near Gudur for the Andhra Pradesh Furniture Mega Cluster (APFMC). This ambitious project, a public-private partnership, aims to attract ₹3,500 crore in investments and create 50,000 to 70,000 jobs. The cluster will cover the entire production chain, from raw timber to finished furniture, positioning Andhra Pradesh as a global manufacturing and export hub.

news.title: Gujarat RERA seeks details on timeline extensions for government projects
news.para: GujRERA is seeking precedents from the state's R&B department regarding extensions for government civil works due to the West Asia crisis. This comes as real estate associations, citing rising input costs and supply uncertainty, have formally requested a six-month extension for ongoing projects under force majeure provisions.
